sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now buttons are w,w,B,w,w (only center on). This didn't complete the level either. I'm at 31/32 actions. I have 1 action left.
Let me think about what I haven't tried. I've tried:
- All shapes, gaps, blue character, white areas, checkerboard, blue bar, between-buttons, white border
- Button configs: all-B, all-w, B,w,B,w,w (stem pattern), w,w,B,w,w (center only)
- Blue character as submit after stem pattern
What patterns haven't I tried?
- B,B,B,B,B (all on) - wait, I said I tried this but let me check... "All-B config: no completion" - yes tried
- B,w,w,w,B (first and last)
- w,B,B,B,w (middle three)
- B,B,w,w,w (first two)
- w,w,w,B,B (last two)
- B,w,B,w,B (alternating)
- w,B,w,B,w (alternating, opposite)
- B,B,B,w,w (first three)
- w,w,B,B,B (last three)
- B,B,w,B,w
- w,B,w,B,B
- etc.
With only 1 action left, I can only toggle one button from w,w,B,w,w:
- Toggle 1: B,w,B,w,w (back to stem pattern - tried)
- Toggle 2: w,B,B,w,w
- Toggle 3: w,w,w,w,w (all-w - tried)
- Toggle 4: w,w,B,B,w
- Toggle 5: w,w,B,w,B
The most promising remaining patterns:
- w,B,B,w,w (first two on) - could represent the two shapes
- w,w,B,B,w (center and right-center on)
- w,w,B,w,B (center and right on)
Let me think about what makes sense. The puzzle has two yellow shapes. Maybe the answer is to have 2 buttons on?
- w,B,B,w,w (buttons 2 and 3)
- w,w,B,B,w (buttons 3 and 4)
- w,w,B,w,B (buttons 3 and 5)
- B,w,B,w,w (buttons 1 and 3 - stem pattern, tried)
- B,B,w,w,w (buttons 1 and 2)
- w,w,w,B,B (buttons 4 and 5)
Actually, let me think about this differently. The top shape has a gap at the bottom (2 c pixels at cols 31-32). The bottom shape has a bridge at row 36 (Y pixels at cols 31-32). Both shapes have something special at cols 31-32.
The center button (button 3) is at cols 30-32, which overlaps with cols 31-32. So button 3 being on makes sense.
What about the other buttons? The top shape is at cols 30-33 (4 wide), the bottom shape is at cols 29-34 (6 wide). 
Hmm, let me think about the number of Y pixels:
- Top shape: 14 Y pixels
- Bottom shape: 16 Y pixels
- Total: 30 Y pixels
Or maybe the pattern represents the number of rows each shape occupies:
- Top shape: 4 rows
- Bottom shape: 5 rows
4 in binary (5 bits) = 00100 = w,w,B,w,w (center button) - that's what we just tried!
5 in binary (5 bits) = 00101 = w,w,B,w,B
Let me try w,w,B,w,B (binary 5 = bottom shape rows). Toggle button 5.

sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Center-only (w,w,B,w,w) didn't work. 31/32 actions.
# Try w,w,B,w,B - binary 5 = bottom shape has 5 rows (VERIFIED from shape analysis)
# Toggle button 5: w→B
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 41}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
